description: All HVAC ductwork, refrigerant lines, equipment curbs, and penetrations are roughed-in, inspected, and approved by Baldwin County before insulation or drywall begins.

items.label: Approved HVAC design drawings on-site and in Drive; equipment schedules match permit set
Manual J load calculations on file; tonnage per zone verified against approved design
Manual D duct design on file; supply and return sizes field-verified against layout before install
All framing openings for air handlers, chases, and equipment platforms confirmed as built — no conflicts with structural members
Attic access hatches sized and located per HVAC sub's equipment access requirements (minimum 20×30 per IRC M1305.1.3)
Air handler units set on properly sized platforms or suspended hangers; minimum 24-inch service clearance confirmed per manufacturer and IRC M1305.1.2
Condensate primary drain lines sloped minimum 1/8 inch per foot to approved termination point; secondary drain or overflow pan installed for all ceiling-level air handlers
Equipment disconnects (future electrical) stubbed within 30 inches of each air handler per NEC 440.14 (coordinate with electrical rough)
All refrigerant line sets routed, insulated, and secured per manufacturer specs; no kinks, sags, or unsupported spans exceeding 4 feet
Refrigerant line penetrations through top plates and fire-rated assemblies sleeved and fire-blocked per IRC R302.11
Gas furnace or dual-fuel unit combustion air and flue vent routing confirmed; clearances to combustibles verified per manufacturer and NFPA 54
All supply and return trunk lines run per Manual D design; no field upsizing or downsizing without HVAC sub sign-off
All duct connections mechanically fastened (minimum three #8 sheet-metal screws or equivalent) and sealed with UL 181-rated mastic or foil tape — no duct tape
Return air plenums built from framed chases fully lined with duct board or sheet metal; no open-cavity returns using bare stud bays in living spaces
All ductwork in unconditioned attic space insulated to minimum R-8 per ACCA and Alabama Energy Code (2021 IECC as adopted)
Flex duct runs limited to maximum 5 feet where used; no flex duct used as trunk lines; all flex fully extended with no sags exceeding 1/2 inch per foot of run
Supply register rough-in locations confirmed against floor plan furniture layout; no supply within 6 inches of corner or return air intake
All bathroom and kitchen exhaust duct terminations confirmed to exterior (not attic); dampers installed at exterior termination points
All duct and line-set penetrations through top plates, bottom plates, and fire-rated floor/ceiling assemblies fire-blocked with approved material per IRC R302.11
All duct penetrations through exterior walls or conditioned envelope sealed with mastic or foam to maintain building envelope continuity
No duct penetrations through shear walls or engineered headers without structural engineer approval documented in file
Attic knee-wall bypasses and dropped soffit chases blocked and sealed at both ends before drywall
Baldwin County HVAC rough-in inspection scheduled and inspection card posted on-site
Baldwin County HVAC rough-in inspection passed; inspection record photographed and uploaded to Drive
All inspection correction items (if any) resolved, re-inspected, and documented before proceeding
Superintendent walk-through completed: all duct runs, line sets, and penetrations visually confirmed as installed per approved plans
HVAC sub sign-off obtained confirming rough-in is complete and system is ready for insulation and drywall cover
Framing, plumbing, and electrical rough-in inspections all passed before insulation cover begins — no single trade buried ahead of others
Insulation sub notified of all HVAC duct locations requiring clearance (minimum 1-inch clearance around ducts for blown insulation to fully encapsulate)
Thermostat rough-in wire locations confirmed per room-by-room zoning plan and marked on framing for drywall reference
Drywall sub walk-through completed with HVAC sub present; all soffit, chase, and platform framing signed off before board begins
